justification: All 7 required rules identified with correct invariant classification. Notification rule (BR-10) correctly marked as SOFT/invariant:false. Sanctions screening (BR-7) included as hard invariant. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit reset explicitly surfaced in edge cases and escalation items. 12 acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 82 is within the 60-85 acceptable range with comprehensive assumptions listed.

observations.text: All 10 business rules identified — exceeds the 7 minimum, with correct hard/soft classification throughout
Notification rule (BR-10) correctly classified as SOFT with explicit rationale that notification failure should not block transactions
Sanctions screening (BR-7) correctly identified as hard invariant with detailed acceptance criteria including audit trail and generic rejection message
Daily limit timezone ambiguity explicitly called out in edge cases table and escalation items
12 detailed Given/When/Then acceptance criteria — far exceeds the 4 minimum
Excellent edge case coverage including race conditions, structuring detection, sanctions list updates mid-flight, and exact boundary testing
Properly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified limits without conflating them (BR-4 vs BR-5)
AC-12 tests the exact $1,000 boundary — shows careful attention to threshold semantics
7 concrete escalation items for PM with clear stance that implementation should not proceed on ambiguous items
Confidence level of 82 is slightly above the ideal 65-80 range given the number of unresolved ambiguities listed, though still within acceptable 60-85 band

# Domain Analysis — Digital Wallet Transfer System

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context

This encodes a **digital wallet** system supporting peer-to-peer and entity-to-entity money transfers with regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), fraud prevention (account locking), and risk management (daily limits, settlement finality). The system operates in a regulated financial services context where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.
